ui.noCompare: No comparable events in this window.
ui.seedNote: Seed data is for MVP iteration; dates/locations are approximate.

rulerBio.qinshihuang: First emperor of a unified Qin; standardized systems and central authority.
rulerBio.qiner: Second Qin ruler; succession crisis and rebellions accelerated Qin's collapse.
rulerBio.hangaozu: Founder of the Han dynasty; rebuilt governance after the Qin–Chu wars.
rulerBio.hanwudi: Expansion, state monopolies, and Confucian statecraft shaped the Han imperial model.

rulerHighlight.qinshihuang: Unification, standardization (weights, measures, writing), and commandery-county administration.
rulerHighlight.qiner: Court intrigue and uprisings; Qin rule ends shortly after.
rulerHighlight.hangaozu: Consolidation after Chu–Han contention; early Han institutions take shape.
rulerHighlight.hanwudi: Territorial expansion and institutional reforms; long, influential reign.

sa-770-zhou-east.title: Zhou court moves east to Luoyi (Luoyang)
sa-770-zhou-east.summary: The royal court relocates east after turmoil, marking the conventional start of the Spring and Autumn period.

sa-656-qihuan.title: Duke Huan of Qi becomes hegemon
sa-656-qihuan.summary: Qi rises as a leading state, associated with Guan Zhong's reforms and the hegemon system.

sa-632-chengpu.title: Battle of Chengpu
sa-632-chengpu.summary: Jin defeats Chu in a pivotal battle, shaping the balance among competing states.

sa-551-confucius.title: Birth of Confucius (trad.)
sa-551-confucius.summary: Traditionally dated to this year; later central to East Asian ethical and political thought.

sa-506-boju.title: Battle of Boju
sa-506-boju.summary: Wu defeats Chu and briefly captures its capital, highlighting volatile interstate rivalry.

ws-453-three-jin.title: Partition of Jin (Three Jin)
ws-453-three-jin.summary: Power consolidates into Han, Zhao, and Wei—accelerating the Warring States order.

ws-356-shangyang-reform.title: Shang Yang reforms in Qin
ws-356-shangyang-reform.summary: Legalist reforms strengthen Qin institutions, military organization, and agriculture.

ws-341-maling.title: Battle of Maling
ws-341-maling.summary: Qi defeats Wei with ambush tactics; remembered for Sun Bin's strategy.

ws-260-changping.title: Battle of Changping
ws-260-changping.summary: Qin's victory over Zhao signals dominance and foreshadows imperial unification.

ws-221-unification.title: Qin unifies the warring states
ws-221-unification.summary: Qin conquers rival states; Ying Zheng proclaims himself First Emperor.

qin-214-lingqu.title: Lingqu Canal constructed (trad.)
qin-214-lingqu.summary: A major canal project supports logistics and consolidation in the south.

qin-210-first-emperor-death.title: Death of Qin Shi Huang
qin-210-first-emperor-death.summary: Succession crises and revolts soon follow, undermining Qin rule.

qin-209-dazexiang.title: Dazexiang Uprising
qin-209-dazexiang.summary: A rebellion led by Chen Sheng and Wu Guang becomes a symbol of anti-Qin unrest.

han-202-han-founded.title: Liu Bang founds the Han dynasty
han-202-han-founded.summary: After the Chu–Han contention, Liu Bang becomes Emperor Gaozu and establishes Han.

han-154-seven-states.title: Rebellion of the Seven States
han-154-seven-states.summary: A major revolt by regional kings is suppressed, strengthening central authority.

han-141-wudi.title: Emperor Wu of Han begins reign
han-141-wudi.summary: A transformative reign marked by expansion and institutional change.

han-138-zhangqian.title: Zhang Qian sent to the Western Regions
han-138-zhangqian.summary: Diplomatic missions open routes and knowledge that later underpin Silk Road connections.

han-60-protectorate.title: Protectorate of the Western Regions established
han-60-protectorate.summary: Han administration in the Tarim Basin formalizes influence on key corridors.

eh-105-paper.title: Cai Lun improves papermaking (trad.)
eh-105-paper.summary: Papermaking improvements support administration and cultural diffusion.

eh-184-yellow-turbans.title: Yellow Turban Rebellion
eh-184-yellow-turbans.summary: A massive uprising weakens central control and accelerates fragmentation.

eh-220-han-ends.title: End of Eastern Han
eh-220-han-ends.summary: Emperor Xian abdicates; Cao Pi founds Wei, beginning the Three Kingdoms era.
